identity.name: Product
identity.definition: A Product is an individual Renaissance application that can be included in a Solution or assigned independently.

representations.heading: Card layout
representations.description: How a Product appears in catalogs and selection interfaces

attributes.name: Subject
attributes.description: Primary subject area the Product addresses.

attributes.name: Product Type
attributes.description: Category of product: assessment, practice, reading, or instruction.

representations.heading: List layout
representations.description: How a Product appears in Solution detail views and assignment tables

attributes.name: Availability
attributes.description: Current availability: available, deprecated, or sunset.

attributes.name: Product Type
attributes.description: Category of product: assessment, practice, reading, or instruction.

representations.heading: Detail page layout
representations.description: What users see when they open a full Product record

attributes.name: Product ID
attributes.description: Unique system identifier for this Product.

attributes.name: Apps Tag
attributes.description: Machine-readable tag used in the apps platform.

attributes.name: Availability
attributes.description: Current availability: available, deprecated, or sunset.

attributes.name: Subject
attributes.description: Primary subject area: Reading, Math, SEL, All.

attributes.name: Product Type
attributes.description: Category: assessment, practice, reading, or instruction.

stories.title: Browse Product Catalog
stories.benefit: I can understand which individual applications are available and how they relate to Solutions.
stories.whenClause: I navigate to the Product catalog,
stories.thenClause: I see all Products with their types, subjects, tiers, and availability status.

businessRules.title: Uniqueness
businessRules.description: A Product is uniquely identified by its Product ID. The Apps Tag must also be unique across all Products.

businessRules.title: Solution Membership
businessRules.description: A Product can belong to zero or more Solutions. Products can also be assigned independently via Product Assignments.

businessRules.title: Availability Transitions
businessRules.description: Available Products can be deprecated (no new assignments). Deprecated Products can be sunset (fully removed). Sunset Products cannot be restored.

businessRules.title: Student Access
businessRules.description: A Student's access to a Product is computed from the Product Assignments that apply to their school, grade, and class. Direct Product-to-Student access is derived, not stored.

businessRules.title: Deprecation
businessRules.description: Deprecated Products remain accessible to existing users but cannot be included in new Solution configurations or new Product Assignments.

businessRules.title: Cross-Product Data
businessRules.description: Activity Events and Scores reference a Product as their source. Product identity is preserved in historical data even after deprecation or sunset.

states.name: Available
states.description: Product is active and can be assigned or included in Solutions

states.name: Deprecated
states.description: Product is being phased out; existing access continues but no new assignments

states.name: Sunset
states.description: Product is fully removed from the platform
states.triggers: End-of-life date reached after deprecation period

relationships.mechanics: Student access to a Product is computed from Product Assignments that apply to the Student's school, grade, and class. There is no direct Student-Product link stored.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-many (computed). A Product is accessible to many Students; a Student can access many Products.
relationships.sorts: Students accessing a Product: by school, by grade, by name.
relationships.filters: By school, by grade, by class, by enrollment status.
relationships.dependencies: If a Product is deprecated, existing Student access is maintained until the corresponding Product Assignment is deactivated.

allAttributes.name: Product ID
allAttributes.description: Unique system identifier for this Product.

allAttributes.name: Name
allAttributes.description: Display name of the Product. Must be unique across all Products.

allAttributes.name: Apps Tag
allAttributes.description: Machine-readable tag used in the apps platform for routing and identification.

allAttributes.name: Tier
allAttributes.description: Product tier level for licensing and commercial grouping.

allAttributes.name: Availability
allAttributes.description: Current availability: available, deprecated, or sunset.

allAttributes.name: Included Solutions
allAttributes.description: Solutions that include this Product. Computed from Solution configurations.

allAttributes.name: Subject
allAttributes.description: Primary subject area: Reading, Math, SEL, Science, or All.

allAttributes.name: Product Type
allAttributes.description: Category of product: assessment, practice, reading, or instruction.

structure.evidence: A Product has meaningful attributes: name, apps tag, tier, availability, subject, product type, and Solution memberships. A detail page can be designed.

instances.evidence: Star Reading (Premium, assessment, Reading), Freckle (Standard, practice, Math), myON (Standard, reading, Reading), Nearpod (Standard, instruction, All). Renaissance has ~15 distinct Product instances.

purpose.evidence: Products are the individual applications that Students and Teachers use daily. They are the atomic unit of Renaissance's software portfolio, referenced by Solutions, Product Assignments, Activity Events, and Scores.

sipValidation.verdict: Domain object. The atomic application unit that Solutions bundle and Product Assignments provision.

synonyms.notes: Short form used in technical contexts and the apps platform
Full form used in legal and commercial contexts
Used when describing Products as classroom tools

shapeshifterMatrix.description: Products shown as cards with tier, subject, and type. Admins browse the catalog to understand what's available and drill into product details.
Products listed within a solution's detail page, showing tier and availability. Admins see which products are bundled in a given solution.
The complete product record — ID, tier, included solutions, subject, and availability. Admins review product configuration and component details.
